No Poverty.reason: Analyzes historical "economic... changes" and "political and economic transformation," which are policy frameworks impacting poverty, linking to Target 1.b.

Zero Hunger.reason: The course covers historical periods like "War Communism," which involved policies that led to famine, indirectly relating to Target 2.1 (end hunger).

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The course covers multiple wars ("Great Patriotic War", "Chechen War"), which have significant health impacts, a very indirect link to well-being.

Quality Education.reason: The course is tertiary education (Target 4.3) and aims to provide an "international macro vision," fostering knowledge for global citizenship (Target 4.7).

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: Explicitly analyzes "economic... changes," "socialist construction," and "political and economic transformation," which are central to economic growth and employment policies (Targets 8.1, 8.2).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: Covers "socialist construction" and the "Stalinist model," which were defined by rapid, state-led industrialization and infrastructure development, linking to Targets 9.1 and 9.2.

Reduced Inequalities.reason: Analyzes the "Russian Revolution," a response to inequality, and explicitly covers "nationality policy," directly addressing ethnic inclusion and discrimination (Targets 10.2, 10.3).

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course is fundamentally about political transformation, conflict, and institution-building, covering revolutions, wars, the Cold War, and the creation/collapse of states (Targets 16.1, 16.6).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: Examines international relations through the "Cold War" and the "Commonwealth of Independent States," linking to global partnerships and stability (Targets 17.13, 17.16).
